question_text:
Please provide me with the Kubernetes ConfigMap configuration for the game-demo application.
It specifies the initial number of lives for a player is "3", the ui_properties_file_name is "user-interface.properties", 
The game.properties specifies the params of 2 object: enemy and player. the types of enemies in the game are 'aliens,monsters'.
the maximum-lives a player can have is 5.
question_simplified_text: Provide Kubernetes ConfigMap config for game-demo app. Initial player lives: 3, ui_properties_file_name: user-interface.properties. game.properties defines params for objects enemy and player. Enemy types: aliens, monsters. Max player lives: 5.
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: game-demo
data:
  player_initial_lives: "3"
  ui_properties_file_name: "user-interface.properties"
  game.properties: |
    enemy.types=aliens,monsters
    player.maximum-lives=5
target_yaml_normalized:
apiVersion: v1
data:
  game.properties: 'enemy.types=aliens,monsters

    player.maximum-lives=5'
  player_initial_lives: '3'
  ui_properties_file_name: user-interface.properties
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: game-demo

question_text:
Please write a yaml file for k8s cronjob that prints 'OK' every minute using the latest ubuntu image. 
The name of the job has to be 'example'.
question_simplified_text: Write a YAML for a k8s cronjob that prints 'OK' every minute using the latest ubuntu image. Job name: 'example'.
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: example
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: example
            image: ubuntu:latest
            command: ["bash", "-c", "echo 'OK'"]
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
target_yaml_normalized:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: example
spec:
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - command:
            - bash
            - -c
            - echo 'OK'
            image: ubuntu:latest
            name: example
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
  schedule: '*/1 * * * *'

question_text:
Is there a way to automatically remove completed Jobs besides making a CronJob to clean up completed Jobs?
Specifically, Please modify this YAML so that it can only keep 0 completed jobs and 2 failed job. 
```
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: hello
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: hello
            image: busybox
            args:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - date; echo Hello from the Kubernetes cluster
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
```
question_simplified_text:
Can we automatically remove completed Jobs without using a CronJob? Modify this YAML to keep 0 completed jobs and 2 failed jobs.
```
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: hello
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: hello
            image: busybox
            args:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - date; echo Hello from the Kubernetes cluster
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
```
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: hello
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  successfulJobsHistoryLimit: 0
  failedJobsHistoryLimit: 2
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: hello
            image: busybox
            args:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - date; echo Hello from the Kubernetes cluster
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
target_yaml_normalized:

question_text:
Create a Kubernetes CronJob to run the following job every minute. 
If an existing job is running, it will be replaced with a new one. 
You can use any images, such as busybox.
```
/bin/sh -c "sleep 65"
```
question_simplified_text: Create a k8s CronJob to run a job every minute. Replace existing job if running. Use any image, e.g. busybox. Job command: `/bin/sh -c "sleep 65"`.
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: x-job
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  concurrencyPolicy: Replace
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: busybox
            image: busybox
            args:
            - "/bin/sh"
            - "-c"
            - "sleep 65"
          restartPolicy: Never
target_yaml_normalized:

  name: x-job

  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:

          - args:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - sleep 65
            image: busybox
            name: busybox
          restartPolicy: Never
  schedule: '*/1 * * * *'

question_text:
Please use Kubernetes YAML to set up a CronJob that runs every minute. 
The job should run a busybox container which outputs the value of an environment variable CRON_NAME. 
Name the CronJob "env-job" and set the environment variable's value to CronTest.
question_simplified_text: Create a Kubernetes CronJob YAML that runs every minute. The job should use a busybox container to output the value of the environment variable CRON_NAME. Name the CronJob "env-job" and set the environment variable's value to CronTest.
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  name: env-job
spec:
  schedule: "*/1 * * * *"
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - name: busybox
            image: busybox:latest
            command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "echo $CRON_NAME"]
            env:
            - name: CRON_NAME
              value: "CronTest"
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
target_yaml_normalized:

  name: env-job
spec:
  jobTemplate:
    spec:
      template:
        spec:
          containers:
          - command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - echo $CRON_NAME
            env:
            - name: CRON_NAME
              value: CronTest
            image: busybox:latest
            name: busybox
          restartPolicy: OnFailure
  schedule: '*/1 * * * *'

question_text:
Craft a yaml file to define a k8s DaemonSet named "ds-hostpath". The DaemonSet should employ the latest version of the nginx image. 
The DaemonSet's metadata must be labeled with "app: ds-hostpath". Furthermore, nginx logs need to be mounted to the node's 
/var/log/nginx directory.Ensure using the DirectoryOrCreate option for this purpose.
question_simplified_text: Create a yaml for a k8s DaemonSet named "ds-hostpath". Use the latest nginx image. Label the DaemonSet's metadata with "app: ds-hostpath". Mount nginx logs to the node's /var/log/nginx directory using the DirectoryOrCreate option.
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: ds-hostpath
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: ds-hostpath
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: ds-hostpath
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:latest
        volumeMounts:
        - name: nginx-logs
          mountPath: /var/log/nginx
      volumes:
      - name: nginx-logs
        hostPath:
          path: /var/log/nginx
          type: DirectoryOrCreate
target_yaml_normalized:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: ds-hostpath
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: ds-hostpath
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: ds-hostpath
    spec:
      containers:
      - image: nginx:latest
        name: nginx
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /var/log/nginx
          name: nginx-logs
      volumes:
      - hostPath:
          path: /var/log/nginx
          type: DirectoryOrCreate
        name: nginx-logs

question_text:
Design a Kubernetes yaml file for a DaemonSet with the following specifications:
1. The template metadata label the pods with the name "fluentd-elasticsearch".
2. It should contain a single container named "fluentd-elasticsearch" that uses the image quay.io/fluentd_elasticsearch/fluentd:v2.5.2.
3. This container should have specific resource requirements:
    - Set a memory limit of 200Mi.
    - Request 100m of CPU and 200Mi of memory.
question_simplified_text:
Design a DaemonSet YAML with the following specs:
1. Pods labeled "fluentd-elasticsearch".
2. Single container named "fluentd-elasticsearch" using image quay.io/fluentd_elasticsearch/fluentd:v2.5.2.
3. Container resource requirements:
   - Memory limit: 200Mi.
   - CPU request: 100m, Memory request: 200Mi.

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: fluentd-elasticsearch
  labels:
    k8s-app: fluentd-logging
spec:

      name: fluentd-elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: fluentd-elasticsearch
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: fluentd-elasticsearch
        image: quay.io/fluentd_elasticsearch/fluentd:v2.5.2

            cpu: 100m
            memory: 200Mi

          containerPort: 9445
          hostPort: 9445
      hostNetwork: true

  name: exporter-modified # *
  labels:
    component: exporter-modified

      port: 9445
      protocol: TCP
  selector:

question_text:
I have a Service Account (kettle) on minikube. Create a Deployment (kettle) and a PersistentVolumeClaim (kettle-data).
The Deployment has one replica (kettle). Use the SA kettle in the Deployment. Label the Pod with the key app and value kettle. 
The Deployment should have one container (kettle). Use the image busybox. 
The container should have two environment variables: DEPLOYMENT with the value prod; SUBSCRIPTION_PATH with the value local-subscription. The container should mount a volume at /data from the PVC kettle-data. 
The container runs the commands: "/bin/sh -c sleep 600".
question_simplified_text:
Create a Deployment (kettle) and a PersistentVolumeClaim (kettle-data) using the Service Account (SA) kettle on minikube. 
Deployment (kettle) has 1 replica and uses the SA kettle. 
Label the Pod with app:kettle. 
Deployment (kettle) has 1 container using the image busybox. 
Container has 2 environment variables: DEPLOYMENT=prod and SUBSCRIPTION_PATH=local-subscription. 
Container mounts a volume at /data from PVC kettle-data. 
Container runs the command: "/bin/sh -c sleep 600".
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: kettle
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: kettle-data
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: kettle
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: kettle
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: kettle
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: kettle
      containers:
      - name: kettle
        image: busybox
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 600"]
        env:
        - name: DEPLOYMENT
          value: prod
        - name: SUBSCRIPTION_PATH
          value: local-subscription
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data
      volumes:
      - name: data
        persistentVolumeClaim:
          claimName: kettle-data

question_text:
Create a YAML file for a Deployment called custom-deploy. The Deployment should use the nginx:latest image, have 1 replica, and expose port 80. 
Additionally, set the terminationGracePeriodSeconds to 10 seconds and add a preStop lifecycle hook that executes a command to sleep for 20 seconds.
A Service configuration is provided as follow. Just attach it to the beginning of your answer.
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: nginx
  ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
```
question_simplified_text:
Create a YAML for a Deployment named custom-deploy. Use nginx:latest image, 1 replica, expose port 80. Set terminationGracePeriodSeconds to 10s and add a preStop lifecycle hook with a command to sleep for 20s. Attach the following Service configuration at the beginning of your answer:
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: nginx
  ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
```
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: nginx
  ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: custom-deploy
  labels:
    app: nginx
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 10
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
        lifecycle:
          preStop:
            exec:
              command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "sleep 20"]

  name: nginx-service

  - port: 80
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 80
  selector:
    app: nginx
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment

  labels:
    app: nginx
  name: custom-deploy
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  template:

question_text:
How do I configure a k8s deployment (nginx-deploy-2) that should only be scheduled on nodes that have a disk type of SSD. 
Use node affinity to accomplish this. As an example, app:web can be used as label and nginx can be used as image.
question_simplified_text: Configure a k8s deployment (nginx-deploy-2) to schedule only on nodes with SSD disk type using node affinity. Use label app:web and image nginx as an example.
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-deploy-2
  labels:
    app: web
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web
  replicas: 5
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web
    spec:
      affinity:
        nodeAffinity:
          requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
            nodeSelectorTerms:
            - matchExpressions:
              - key: disktype
                operator: In
                values:
                - ssd
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80

question_text:
You need to set up a Kubernetes Job that will spawn 3 parallel pods (example-job). These pods should be able to communicate with each other using DNS. To facilitate this, you also need to create a headless service (headless-svc). The job's pods should ping each other by their DNS names (script provided). Use the bash:latest image for the job's container.
```
for i in 0 1 2
do
  gotStatus="-1"
  wantStatus="0"             
  while [ $gotStatus -ne $wantStatus ]
  do                                       
    ping -c 1 example-job-${i}.headless-svc > /dev/null 2>&1
    gotStatus=$?                
    if [ $gotStatus -ne $wantStatus ]; then
      echo "Failed to ping pod example-job-${i}.headless-svc, retrying in 1 second..."
      sleep 1
    fi
  done                                                         
  echo "Successfully pinged pod: example-job-${i}.headless-svc"
done
```
question_simplified_text:
Set up a Kubernetes Job (example-job) with 3 parallel pods. Pods should communicate using DNS. Create a headless service (headless-svc) for DNS resolution. Pods should ping each other using their DNS names. Use the bash:latest image for the job's container.
```
for i in 0 1 2
do
  gotStatus="-1"
  wantStatus="0"             
  while [ $gotStatus -ne $wantStatus ]
  do                                       
    ping -c 1 example-job-${i}.headless-svc > /dev/null 2>&1
    gotStatus=$?                
    if [ $gotStatus -ne $wantStatus ]; then
      echo "Failed to ping pod example-job-${i}.headless-svc, retrying in 1 second..."
      sleep 1
    fi
  done                                                         
  echo "Successfully pinged pod: example-job-${i}.headless-svc"
done
```
target_yaml_raw:

  name: headless-svc
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    job-name: example-job
---
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: example-job
spec:
  completions: 3
  parallelism: 3
  completionMode: Indexed
  template:
    spec:
      subdomain: headless-svc
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: example-workload
        image: bash:latest
        command:
        - bash
        - -c
        - |
          for i in 0 1 2
          do
            gotStatus="-1"
            wantStatus="0"             
            while [ $gotStatus -ne $wantStatus ]
            do                                       
              ping -c 1 example-job-${i}.headless-svc > /dev/null 2>&1
              gotStatus=$?                
              if [ $gotStatus -ne $wantStatus ]; then
                echo "Failed to ping pod example-job-${i}.headless-svc, retrying in 1 second..."
                sleep 1
              fi
            done                                                         
            echo "Successfully pinged pod: example-job-${i}.headless-svc"
          done

  - name: sec-ctx-vol

  containers:
  - name: sec-ctx-demo

- All processes of the container cannot gain more privileges than its parent process.

spec:
  volumes:
  - name: sec-ctx-vol

How can you configure a Pod so that it uses values from a ConfigMap in its container's command arguments?

apiVersion: v1

To perform a probe, the kubelet sends an HTTP GET request to the server that is running in the container and listening on port 8080. 
If the handler for the server's /healthz path returns a success code, the kubelet considers the container to be alive and healthy. 
If the handler returns a failure code, the kubelet kills the container and restarts it.

    args:

Please create an pod with "nginx" image named "lifecycle-demo" with full lifecycle.
Specifically, it should create a file /usr/share/message, which contains a line of chars: "Hello from the postStart handler".
It should also shuts down nginx gracefully after it stops for whatever reasons, in other word, execute "nginx -s quit; while killall -0 nginx; do sleep 1; done"
question_simplified_text:
Create a pod named "lifecycle-demo" with the "nginx" image. Include full lifecycle. 
Create a file /usr/share/message with the line "Hello from the postStart handler". 
Ensure nginx shuts down gracefully by executing "nginx -s quit; while killall -0 nginx; do sleep 1; done" after it stops.
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: lifecycle-demo
spec:
  containers:
  - name: lifecycle-demo-container # *
    image: nginx
    lifecycle:
      postStart:
        exec:
          command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "echo Hello from the postStart handler > /usr/share/message"]
      preStop:
        exec:
          command: ["/bin/sh","-c","nginx -s quit; while killall -0 nginx; do sleep 1; done"]

        exec:

    name: lifecycle-demo-container

question_text:
Can you create a YAML configuration file for a Kubernetes ReplicationController that deploys 5 replicas of a pod? Each pod should have:
- A host network and host PID enabled
- A privileged container running the alpine image (name: agent)
- Four host volumes (/dev, /proc, /usr) mounted into /host/[corresponding-path] inside the container. All should be read-only.
- You can use "while true; do sleep 10; done" to keep them alive.
question_simplified_text:
Create a YAML for a k8s ReplicationController deploying 5 replicas of a pod. Each pod should have:
- Host network and host PID enabled
- Privileged container running alpine image (name: agent)
- Four host volumes (/dev, /proc, /usr) mounted into /host/[corresponding-path] inside the container. All should be read-only.
- Use "while true; do sleep 10; done" to keep them alive.
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ReplicationController
metadata:
  name: test-2
  labels:
    app: test-2
spec:
  replicas: 5
  selector:
    app: agent-test
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: agent-test
    spec:
      volumes:
      - name: dev-vol
        hostPath:
          path: /dev
      - name: proc-vol
        hostPath:
          path: /proc
      - name: usr-vol
        hostPath:
          path: /usr
      hostNetwork: true
      hostPID: true
      containers:
      - name: agent
        image: alpine
        command: ["sh", "-c", "while true; do sleep 10; done"]
        securityContext:
          privileged: true
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /host/dev
          name: dev-vol
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /host/proc
          name: proc-vol
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /host/usr
          name: usr-vol
          readOnly: true

        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /host/dev
          name: dev-vol
          readOnly: true
        - mountPath: /host/proc

        - mountPath: /host/usr

metadata:
  name: secret-dotfiles-pod
spec:
  volumes:
    - name: secret-volume
      secret:
        secretName: dotfile-secret
  containers:
    - name: dotfile-test-container

      command:

question_text:
I want to create a headless Service, nginx, to publish the IP addresses of 2 Pods in a StatefulSet, web. 
Specially,I want all Pods to be launched or terminated in parallel. 
Each container should be running the latest nginx image, and use port 80 named "web". 
The volume should be named "www". 
How should I write the yaml that configures both the Service and the StatefulSet? 
You can concatenate them into a single file.
question_simplified_text:
Write a YAML file to configure a headless Service named "nginx" and a StatefulSet named "web". 
The Service should publish the IP addresses of 2 Pods in the StatefulSet. 
All Pods should be launched or terminated in parallel. 
Each container should use the latest nginx image, run on port 80 named "web", and have a volume named "www".
target_yaml_raw:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx
  labels:
    app: nginx
spec:
  ports:
  - port: 80
    name: web
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: nginx
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: web
spec:
  serviceName: "nginx"
  podManagementPolicy: "Parallel"
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
          name: web
        volumeMounts:
        - name: www
          mountPath: /usr/share/nginx/html # *
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: www
    spec:
      accessModes: [ "ReadWriteOnce" ] # *
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 1Gi # *

question_text:
Update the following Kubernetes StatefulSet to have a StorageClass for a Cassandra cluster. The StorageClass should be named "fast" and the provisioner is "k8s.io/minikube-hostpath". For PersistentVolumeClaim, make sure to request 100 MB storage with access mode ReadWriteOnce.
```
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: my-cassandra
  labels:
    app: my-cassandra
spec:
  serviceName: cassandra
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-cassandra
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-cassandra
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: cassandra
        image: gcr.io/google-samples/cassandra:v13
        env:
          - name: MAX_HEAP_SIZE
            value: 512M
          - name: HEAP_NEWSIZE
            value: 100M
```

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: zk
spec:
  serviceName: zk-hs
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: zk
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: zk
    spec:
      affinity:
        podAntiAffinity:
          requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
          - labelSelector:
              matchExpressions:
              - key: "app"
                operator: In
                values:
                - zk
            topologyKey: "kubernetes.io/hostname"

          timeoutSeconds: 5
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - sh
            - -c
            - "zookeeper-ready 2181"
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
